sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, a male in his mid-twenties, presents with neck pain that began after a car crash in February of this year.
He reports experiencing severe pain, rated 12 out of 10, which is sharp and incapacitating.
He has a history of multiple car crashes, including the most recent one in February, which resulted in neck and back pain.
He has been to a pain clinic and has been prescribed fentanyl, but has not taken it in several weeks.
He reports experiencing fatigue, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports experiencing fatigue, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
Current medications: Fentanyl [DOSE NOT MENTIONED] [FREQUENCY NOT MENTIONED] [INDICATION] (stopped taking several weeks ago)
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has limited range of motion in his neck, with pain on palpation on the bony process and muscle.
He is unable to move his neck from side to side or bend it forward and backward without pain.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which is a non-fractured injury to the neck, as evidenced by the absence of a fracture on the imaging results.
Problem list: Neck sprain; suspected neck fracture; [NO OTHER PROBLEMS MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500 mg, 6-8 hours, 6-8 hours for muscle relaxation and pain management.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to evaluate additional steps for treatment.
I will also refer the patient to pain management for further evaluation of local injections and alternative pain management options.
I will order an MRI to obtain a thorough image of the neck and soft tissues.
I will give the patient a note to excuse him from work until the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient after the MRI results are available to discuss further treatment options.
